meta.name: Pozole Rojo
meta.subtitle: Hominy stew with pork (or chicken) and a red chile broth, served with fresh crunchy garnishes

summary: Classic pozole rojo: a comforting hominy stew with tender pork (or chicken), a deeply flavored red chile broth, and a build-your-own garnish tray (cabbage, radish, lime, onion, oregano). Designed for SSA planning: big-batch friendly, reheats well, and garnishes can be prepped separately for speed.

yield.notes: Makes about 12–14 cups total. Scale up easily for events.

optional.label: Pressure Cooker / Instant Pot
optional.notes: Speeds up pork tenderizing; see variations

ingredients.label: Pork shoulder (boneless)
ingredients.notes: Cut into 1.5-inch chunks; may substitute chicken thighs (see variations)

ingredients.label: White onion
ingredients.notes: Half for cooking; half for garnish

ingredients.label: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: Some for broth, some for chile blend

ingredients.label: Hominy, canned (drained and rinsed)
ingredients.notes: Use 25–30 oz cans; or ~6 cups cooked hominy

ingredients.label: Chipotle in adobo
ingredients.notes: Use 1 chopped pepper + a little sauce for smoky heat

steps.title: Prep and start the broth
steps.body: Cut pork into chunks and season with about 1 tsp salt. Add pork to a large pot with half the onion (quartered), 3 cloves garlic, bay leaves (optional), 1 tsp Mexican oregano, and enough water to cover by 2 inches (about 10–12 cups). Bring to a boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer.

steps.title: Simmer until pork is tender
steps.body: Simmer partially covered until pork is fork-tender, about 75–100 minutes. Skim foam as needed. Remove onion/garlic/bay when done (optional).
checks: Pork is tender and easily shreds with a fork

steps.title: Toast and soak dried chiles
steps.body: While the pork simmers, toast guajillo and ancho chiles briefly in a dry skillet (10–20 seconds per side) until fragrant—do not burn. Cover with hot water and soak 15 minutes until pliable.
checks: Chiles are pliable and smell aromatic (not bitter/burnt)

steps.title: Blend the red chile puree
steps.body: Drain soaked chiles (reserve a little soak water). Blend chiles with 3 cloves garlic, 1 tbsp vinegar (optional), and 1–2 cups of broth (or soak water) until very smooth. For an extra-smooth broth, strain through a fine mesh strainer.

steps.title: Bloom the chile puree for deeper flavor
steps.body: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a saucepan or skillet over medium heat. Pour in the chile puree carefully and cook 3–5 minutes, stirring, until darkened slightly and aromatic. Add chipotle in adobo if using.
checks: Puree smells rich, not raw; color deepens slightly

steps.title: Combine broth, chile, and hominy
steps.body: Stir the bloomed chile puree into the pork pot. Add drained and rinsed hominy. Simmer 20–30 minutes to meld flavors. Season with remaining salt to taste; add extra oregano if desired.
checks: Broth tastes balanced: savory + chile depth + bright finish with lime at serving

steps.title: Prepare garnish tray
steps.body: Shred cabbage, slice radishes, chop cilantro, and cut limes into wedges. Dice remaining onion finely. Set out Mexican oregano and any additional toppings you like.

steps.title: Serve
steps.body: Ladle pozole into bowls. Let each person top with cabbage, radish, onion, oregano, cilantro, and lots of lime.

variations.label: Pozole Rojo de Pollo
variations.notes: Use 2–2.5 lb bone-in chicken thighs (or a whole chicken cut up). Simmer until cooked, shred, return to pot. Total time drops to ~60–75 minutes.

variations.label: Pressure Cooker / Instant Pot
variations.notes: Cook pork with aromatics and water/broth for ~35 minutes high pressure + natural release. Then stir in chile puree and hominy; simmer on sauté 15–20 minutes.

variations.label: Milder Pozole
variations.notes: Use fewer chiles (e.g., 4 guajillo + 1 ancho), omit chipotle, and increase broth slightly.

variations.label: Spicier Pozole
variations.notes: Add 1–2 árbol chiles to the soak/blend or increase chipotle in adobo.

storage.refrigeration: Cool and refrigerate pozole (without garnishes) for up to 4 days.
storage.freezing: Freeze stew base (without fresh garnishes) for up to 3 months. Thaw overnight and reheat gently; add water/broth as needed.
storage.reheat: Reheat on the stove until steaming hot. Keep garnish tray separate.

notes: Cool stew within 2 hours before refrigerating.
Reheat leftovers to steaming hot throughout.

prepNotes: Make chile puree up to 2 days ahead and refrigerate.
Prep garnish tray same-day; store cabbage/radish separately for best crunch.
planner.leftoversPlan: Portion stew base into containers; pack garnish cups separately to keep crunch.

notes: Canonical SSA pozole rojo baseline focuses on a dried-chile blend for depth, not chili powder.
If using canned hominy with varying can sizes, target ~6 cups drained hominy.
Flavor is finished at the bowl with lime and oregano—don’t skip the garnish tray.
